nameMeanings.name: Wilcox
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire, Lancashire, and Flintshire): variant of Wilcock , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This form of the surname is based on the pronunciation.

variants.name: Wilcock
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Wilcoc, a pet form of William (composed of the elements Will 1 + the hypocoristic suffix -cok; see Cocke ).

variants.name: Willcox
meanings: English: variant of   Wilcox  , widespread in southwestern England.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Wilcox was born in 1405, in Shropshire, England, her father, William Wilcox, was 23 and her mother, Lucia de Cam, was 35. She had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with William Hayward. She died in 1485, in her hometown, at the age of 80, and was buried in Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Wilcox est née en 1405, à Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, William Wilcox, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Lucia de Cam, avait 35 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec William Hayward. Elle est décédée en 1485, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterrée à Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Wilcox 1405 in Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Wilcox 23 und ihre Mutter Lucia de Cam 35. Sie hatte mit William Hayward mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1485 im Alter von 80 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Shropshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Wilcox è nata il nel 1405 nel Shropshire, England, sua padre, William Wilcox, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Lucia de Cam, aveva 35 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con William Hayward. Morì nel 1485, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 80 anni, e fu sepolta a Shropshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Wilcoxが1405年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアで生まれた時，父William Wilcoxは23歳，母Lucia de Camは35歳であった。彼女はWilliam Haywardとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1485年に故郷で80歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Wilcox(이)가 1405년 영국 슈럽실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Wilcox(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lucia de Cam(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 William Hayward(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1485년 그녀의 고향에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Wilcox nasceu em 1405, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Wilcox, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Lucia de Cam, tinha 35 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com William Hayward. Ela faleceu em 1485, em sua cidade natal, com 80 anos, e foi sepultada em Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Wilcox родилась в 1405 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Wilcox было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Lucia de Cam, 35. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от William Hayward. Она умерла в 1485 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 80 лет и была похоронена в местности шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Wilcox nació en 1405, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Wilcox, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Lucia de Cam, tenía 35 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con William Hayward. Murió en 1485, en su pueblo, a la edad de 80 años, y fue sepultada en Shropshire, Inglaterra.
